sectionTitle: The Heartbreaking Math of the Expat
sectionDescription: Have you ever dared to calculate how many real chances you have left to truly understand your parents before it's too late?

cards.title: The Finite Number 20
cards.description: If you return home once a year, you may have only 20 more meals left to share with your parents. Each passing year is an opportunity lost forever.
cards.quote: I was shocked to realize I had less than 30 real days left with my parents for the rest of their lives.

cards.title: The Fear of 'The Familiar Stranger'
cards.description: Zalo messages still arrive, FaceTime still connects, but you gradually lose track of what your parents are truly thinking. You're losing them while they're still alive.
cards.quote: The scariest thing is coming home and realizing I missed a whole vital chapter of my mother's life.

cards.title: The Empty Echo
cards.description: Messaging apps are just quick communication tools, not treasure chests. One day, you will crave to hear a piece of advice again, but there will only be silence.
cards.quote: Scrolling through old texts isn't the same as hearing the warmth in my father's voice.

emotionalQuote: Our greatest mistake is thinking we still have a lot of time.
